raw_consumer_statement:
This is a follow up to : XXXX the company said was investigating and closed it without providing a reason, nor prove that these charges are someting legit, or I agreed. 

Amex does not protect customer, they just validate transactions, and most schemes are a bit more complex than validity of transaction, for example when they sell you cat for rabbit. The issue or issues is the inaccuracy of of charges, and possible duplicates. These are the facts : I made a reservation for a car via XXXX to confirmation number XXXX for a car at XXXX via XXXX. The reservation is for Saturday XX/XX/XXXX, to Wednesday XX/XX/XXXX. Notice the car was returned Tuesday night. The total was XXXX. They refused to honor the reservation. They offered an alternate tariff, that had some mandatory insurance which made the total XXXX. When I check my American express I had not one but two charges, very different than what was agreed : Snip of contract total : Then I got not one but two questionable charges on XX/XX/XXXX. Notice that the date is XX/XX/XXXX XXXX XXXX I dont know how to explain this. Apparently both have extra fees, things like cleaning/gass.. Mileage is not a problem, as I did not drive a lot. But only if you add an extra day you get to XXXX as gas is not like XXXX a gallon, and adding extradays would make up XXXX ( the car was returned the day before ). It is a bit more than a duplicate. And amex does not investigate anything, or waste time even understanding. I wasted my time explaining multiple times this problem, and you just keep charging my card despite claiming that you protect the customer, and this is not genuine, I should not be responsible for moneys I do not owe.
